content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. The sequence is circular, i.e. for each valid iii, elements AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​ are considered adjacent, and elements A1A_1A1​ and ANA_NAN​ are also considered adjacent. Initially, each element of this sequence is either 111 or 000.

You may perform some operations on this sequence. In one operation, you should choose an index iii such that Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 and move that 111 to one of its adjacent elements, i.e. change AiA_iAi​ to 000, choose one of the elements adjacent to AiA_iAi​ and increment it by 111.

According to Chef, a sequence is good if all its elements are different from 111. You have to tell Chef the minimum number of operations needed to turn the sequence AAA into a good sequence or determine that it is impossible.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (the length of the circular sequence). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, each being 0 or 1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required to make the sequence good, or -1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: no 1s, already good. Test case 2: two 1s, need exactly two operations. Test case 3: three 1s, need exactly three operations.

solution.approach: Let cnt be the number of elements equal to 1. An operation can reduce cnt by at most one (when the chosen 1 is moved onto another element that is already 1 or larger). If cnt = 0 the sequence is already good, answer = 0. If cnt = 1 there is no other 1 to merge with, any move only relocates the single 1, so it is impossible, answer = -1. For cnt >= 2 we can always slide 1s around the circle until two of them become adjacent and merge them, decreasing cnt by one. Repeating this we can eliminate all 1s in exactly cnt operations, which is optimal because each operation can reduce cnt by at most one. Hence answer = cnt for cnt >= 2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case – we only count the number of 1s.
